news.title: Wallabies vs Ireland live: Captain’s try scrubbed out in contentious obstruction call

sections.heading: Vakatawa debuts in midfield against Scotland
sections.body: Former France centre Virimi Vakatawa will make his international return in a Fijian jersey on Saturday after earning 32 caps for Les Bleus. He is named in the midfield alongside Josua Tuisova. Caleb Muntz starts at fly-half, partnering scrum-half Frank Lomani, while Isaiah Armstrong-Ravula takes the No. 15 shirt in a backline primed to challenge the hosts.

sections.heading: No fresh injury news in today's coverage
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news for the Flying Fijians in today's coverage ahead of the Murrayfield test. The South Pacific side has utilized their temporary training base in the UK to build up physical resilience after a punishing opening fortnight, though team management has not provided specific fitness updates.

sections.heading: Scotland make 14 changes for Murrayfield clash
sections.body: Scotland head coach Gregor Townsend has overhauled his starting lineup, retaining only flanker Gregor Brown from the side that lost 42-28 to South Africa. Stafford McDowall captains the team, while prolific wingers Darcy Graham and Duhan van der Merwe return. Mindful of "creating depth" for the future, Townsend is also handing first international starts to Gregor Hiddleston and Freddy Douglas.

sections.heading: Fiji seek first win to lift off bottom
sections.body: Fiji sit at the bottom of the tournament table after heavy defeats to Wales and England, including a 73-8 blowout last weekend. A victory in Edinburgh would break their duck and repeat last July's 29-14 triumph over the Scots. However, another defeat would condemn the Flying Fijians to a disappointing 0-3 start before the campaign pauses until autumn.

sections.heading: George warns England could walk off over racist abuse
sections.body: Captain Jamie George has warned that England could walk off the pitch if players face racist abuse during today's Test. The squad remains on high alert after Asher Opoku-Fordjour and Chandler Cunningham-South were targeted by fans in San Juan a year ago. On the field, head coach Steve Borthwick has named an unchanged starting lineup to build on last week's dominant win.

sections.heading: No injuries reported as England keeps low profile
sections.body: No injuries were reported in today's coverage, allowing England to focus entirely on their tactical preparation. To manage energy and avoid distractions after Wednesday's football World Cup semi-final, the players have kept a low profile and avoided wearing national shirts in public. Borthwick has prioritized consistency, preparing the same starting group to face a passionate home crowd.

sections.heading: Argentina make two changes for England clash
sections.body: Argentina have made two changes to their starting lineup as they look to build on their 35-21 victory over Wales. Head coach Felipe Contepomi, who described rugby union as 'booming' in football-mad Argentina, has shuffled his squad to counter England's set-piece. The Pumas aim to break a five-match losing streak against England, using a noisy home crowd in Santiago del Estero to fuel their performance.

sections.heading: England hunt consecutive wins to climb the table
sections.body: England entered the round seventh in the standings after bouncing back with a 73-8 win against Fiji. A second consecutive victory would push them into the upper half of the table. 'We expect a fantastic atmosphere with passionate home support,' Borthwick said, knowing a road victory is vital to keep pace with the tournament's frontrunners.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Former Bleus centre Virimi Vakatawa switches to Fiji
sections.body: Former France centre Virimi Vakatawa is set to resurrect his international career after switching allegiance to Fiji. The dynamic midfielder, who earned 32 caps for Les Bleus, is debuting for the Pacific islanders this weekend. While French fans will watch his return with a mix of nostalgia and apprehension, his inside knowledge of our national setup adds a fascinating layer to our upcoming autumn clash.

sections.heading: Quiet medical room lets France focus on conditioning
sections.body: Today’s coverage brings no fresh injury updates for the French squad, shifting the camp's focus entirely to conditioning and training intensity. Without any new medical concerns reported in the press, the coaching staff can push ahead with physical preparation. Maintaining peak fitness remains the priority as the players navigate their club duties before the squad reconvenes for the autumn internationals.

sections.heading: Virimi Vakatawa debuts for Fiji against Scotland
sections.body: Fiji have named former French international Virimi Vakatawa to make his debut at centre against Scotland this weekend. The midfield maestro brings 32 caps of elite European experience to a dangerous backline. Facing northern hemisphere opposition first, the Flying Fijians will look to establish their physical, offloading game early, providing a direct preview of the tactical threats France must nullify in Round 4.

sections.heading: France target top-three climb after mixed start
sections.body: Les Bleus sit fourth in the standings heading into the weekend after splitting their opening two matches, including a 42-26 victory over Australia. As the tournament progresses, securing a win in their next fixture against Fiji is vital to keep pressure on the frontrunners. With a positive point difference of 14, another high-scoring performance will be crucial to claiming a top-three spot in the tightly contested table.

sections.heading: Squad depth tested as tournament progress demands adaptation
sections.body: Ireland continues to adjust its depth as the Nations Championship progresses. With key captain Caelan Doris ruled out of the entire tournament, Andy Farrell has had to rely on a heavily depleted squad to navigate these early rounds. Despite the high-profile absences, the coaching staff remains focused on blooding new talent before the upcoming round four clash against Argentina.

sections.heading: Medical staff monitors recovery as key quartet remains sidelined
sections.body: The Irish medical team is managing a heavily reduced group during this inaugural tournament. Captain Caelan Doris and three other key players remain ruled out of the championship. With the squad depleted, coaching staff are focusing on carefully managing training intensity and player loads to prevent further casualties as tactical preparations ramp up ahead of the crucial round four fixture.

sections.heading: Tactical focus shifts to neutralizing late Pumas comebacks
sections.body: With no official team sheet released, Ireland must prepare for a physical battle against Felipe Contepomi's side. The Pumas are tactically dangerous in the second half, known for launch-pad comebacks sustained by their fly-half's clinical penalty kicking. Neutralizing their late-game momentum and staying disciplined under pressure will be the primary focus for Farrell's defensive system.

sections.heading: Ireland sits third as championship race intensifies
sections.body: Ireland goes into the weekend third in the table after winning both of their opening matches. After scoring 69 points and conceding 51 across their first two fixtures, Farrell's men need to maintain their perfect start in round four. Overcoming the Pumas is crucial to staying in contention at the summit of the inaugural tournament.

sections.heading: Quesada makes nine changes for Wallabies clash
sections.body: Italy have made nine changes to their starting lineup to face Australia following consecutive defeats against Japan and New Zealand. With head coach Gonzalo Quesada banned for the match, the refreshed Azzurri side features a new-look combination. Melbourne-born winger Monty Ioane starts against his home nation, looking to build on a remarkable journey that began with a temporary move to Italy.

sections.heading: Cannone ruled out of Perth Test under suspension
sections.body: Lock Niccolò Cannone is ruled out of Saturday's Test against the Wallabies as he begins a four-week suspension. The ban was handed down following a head-butting incident involving New Zealand scrumhalf Cam Roigard during Italy's Round 2 defeat. No fresh injury news is reported in today's coverage, leaving the remainder of the squad's absences down to tactical rotation.

sections.heading: Schmidt names Tizzano for final Test in charge
sections.body: Australia's named team features Perth local Carlo Tizzano starting in place of Fraser McReight as coach Joe Schmidt takes charge of the Wallabies for the final time. The hosts, desperate to snap a six-game losing streak, also hand starts to captain Harry Wilson and winger Max Jorgensen. Looking ahead, the Wallabies will prepare for a two-Test series against Japan in August.

sections.heading: Azzurri chase history to avoid winless window
sections.body: Italy enter the final matchday of the summer window seeking to avoid a third consecutive defeat. Having never beaten the Wallabies on Australian soil, a historic victory in Perth would lift the Azzurri off the bottom of the Northern Hemisphere standings. Conversely, another loss ensures Italy finishes this opening block of fixtures without a point before the tournament resumes in November.

sections.heading: Stafford McDowall captains a vastly changed Scotland side
sections.body: Head coach Gregor Townsend has made 14 changes to his starting lineup to face Fiji. Stafford McDowall captains a side featuring first international starts for Gregor Hiddleston and Freddy Douglas. Mindful of development, Townsend noted, "We've got to have an eye on the future, so the depth that we've created and are still creating, that only comes if you give people opportunities."

sections.heading: Leg infection sidelines squad member Nathan Macbeth
sections.body: Nathan Macbeth is the only squad member ruled out of Saturday's match. The player has been sidelined by a leg infection over the last few days, preventing him from joining the heavily rotated squad. Otherwise, the rest of the touring party is available, allowing head coach Gregor Townsend to offer opportunities to almost everyone who traveled.

sections.heading: Former France international Virimi Vakatawa debuts for Fiji
sections.body: Fiji have named their matchday squad, headlined by a debut for former France centre Virimi Vakatawa. The Flying Fijians are seeking their first tournament win following consecutive defeats to Wales and England — who hammered them 73-8 last weekend. Frank Lomani and Isaiah Armstrong-Ravula start in a backline hoping to replicate Fiji's 29-14 victory over Scotland last July.

sections.heading: Scotland seek crucial response after South Africa defeat
sections.body: Scotland enter the weekend in the middle of the table after split results from their opening matches. Following an opening-round victory in Argentina, Gregor Townsend's side suffered a 28-42 defeat against South Africa last weekend. Securing a second win before the summer window closes is vital to keep pace with leaders Ireland and France in the northern standings.

sections.heading: Tandy overhauls midfield for Durban clash
sections.body: Head coach Steve Tandy has overhauled his backline for the Durban clash, introducing an all-new midfield pairing of Ben Thomas and Max Llewellyn. They are among six changes following the defeat in San Juan, with Louis Rees-Zammit also returning to the starting XV. Tandy has opted for a 6-2 split on the bench, prioritising forward depth to counter the physical threat.

sections.heading: Captain Lake cleared for starting role
sections.body: Captain Dewi Lake has been cleared to lead the side in Durban after initial reports suggested he might miss the Test. His presence is vital for a squad finishing a 10-month campaign that began last August. With the team completing their second long-haul trip in a week, the medical staff is focusing on recovery protocols to maintain intensity against the world champions.

sections.heading: Experimental Boks include four debutants
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has named an experimental lineup featuring 10 changes and four uncapped players: Jaco Williams, Vusi Moyo, Ruben van Heerden, and Carlu Sadie. Assistant coach Mzwandile Stick noted that "Wales are in that same space" as the struggling 2018 Springbok side before their resurgence. Despite the rotations, the hosts retain world-class experience through Pieter-Steph du Toit, who captains from the back row.

sections.heading: Recovering momentum in the North standings
sections.body: Wales goes into the weekend fifth in the Northern Hemisphere standings, level on five points with England but trailing the leading trio. After the disappointment of the 35-21 loss to Argentina, a result today is vital to restore momentum. Facing a South African side that has won five consecutive meetings between these nations, Steve Tandy’s men must defy the odds to avoid sliding.

sections.heading: Contepomi makes two changes to starting XV
sections.body: Felipe Contepomi has freshened his lineup following the victory over Wales, recalling Mayco Vivas to the front row alongside captain Julián Montoya. Gonzalo García earns a start at scrum-half, while the rest of the backline remains settled with Santiago Carreras at fullback. Contepomi believes the sport is in a strong position, noting rugby union is "booming" in football-mad Argentina.

sections.heading: Youthful front row carries heavy conditioning load
sections.body: With no fresh injury news in today's coverage, the medical staff's focus remains on the conditioning of a regenerated front row. The 21-year-old prop Tomás Rapetti retains his starting spot after a physically demanding full debut last week. The coaching staff is closely monitoring the transition of their Under-20 graduates as they face the significant scrummaging pressure expected from the English pack.

sections.heading: Borthwick names unchanged starters for Pumas clash
sections.body: England have named an unchanged starting XV, with Jamie George leading the side for his 113th Test cap. Steve Borthwick has tinkered only with his bench, introducing Ben Spencer and uncapped prop Emmanuel Iyogun. The visitors arrive in Santiago del Estero seeking to extend a five-match winning streak against the Pumas, having avoided wearing national colors in public this week to respect local sensitivities.

sections.heading: Argentina seeks to climb top half of log
sections.body: The Pumas go into the weekend sitting fifth in the overall standings, directly trailing their opponents tonight. While England have historically dominated this rivalry with 24 wins from 30 Tests, Argentina have the momentum of a Round 2 victory and the energy of a historic week in national sport. A win tonight would consolidate their position and potentially leapfrog England in the live table.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Schmidt’s final stand features hometown start
sections.body: Today marks the end of the Joe Schmidt era as the Wallabies coach takes charge for his final Test in Perth. Looking to arrest a six-match losing streak, Schmidt has handed a starting role to local flanker Carlo Tizzano. Tizzano replaces Fraser McReight in a side desperate to secure its first victory of the Nations Championship before Les Kiss takes over the head coaching role.

sections.heading: Availability for the Perth clash
sections.body: While Fraser McReight drops to the bench to accommodate Carlo Tizzano, the remainder of the matchday 23 is fit for selection. There were no new medical withdrawals reported in today's coverage as the squad completed its final preparations in Perth. The team’s focus has been on defensive resilience after conceding 19 defeats in their last 27 international fixtures — including 42 points against France.

sections.heading: Italy overhaul side with nine changes
sections.body: Italy have made nine changes to their starting side as they look to recover from heavy losses to Japan and New Zealand. The Azzurri lineup is bolstered by Australian-born threats, including winger Monty Ioane and Louis Lynagh, both of whom featured in Italy’s 26-19 victory over the Wallabies last year. Despite their recent success in Udine, the Italians have never won a Test match on Australian soil.

sections.heading: Searching for a first win
sections.body: Australia enters the matchday sitting 10th in the overall standings, still searching for a maiden win in the competition. A result today is critical to avoid a winless start to the campaign. Schmidt admitted he is feeling "nervous and a little bit lost" ahead of a fixture that determines if he departs with the worst winning percentage of any Wallabies coach in the professional era.

sections.heading: Strategic reshuffle for French challenge
sections.body: Japan has made five changes to the starting XV for today’s fixture, including a complete overhaul of the front row. Captain Warner Dearns continues to lead the side, joined in the pack by Esei Haangana and Ben Gunter. The coaching staff has looked to inject fresh tactical energy into the lineup, with Takuro Matsunaga and Kazuma Ueda both earning starts to counter the visiting French side.

sections.heading: Conditioning and squad rotation focus
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Japan squad. The focus in the build-up has remained on tactical adjustments and managing the conditioning of the group following the travel from last weekend’s fixture. The bench, bolstered by the inclusion of experienced campaigner Michael Leitch, provides significant cover and late-game impact options for the coaching staff as they seek to sustain intensity.

sections.heading: Les Bleus name heavy-hitting lineup
sections.body: France has confirmed a formidable starting XV featuring an experienced spine of Romain Ntamack, Maxime Lucu, Emmanuel Meafou, and Matthieu Jalibert. Despite recent injury concerns for Jalibert, he has been cleared to start at fullback. The visitors arrive in Tokyo looking to build on their victory over Australia, maintaining the momentum that currently places them among the tournament favorites in the standings.

sections.heading: Finding consistency in Tokyo
sections.body: After securing an opening win against Italy, Japan aims to bounce back from last weekend’s loss to Ireland. The Brave Blossoms currently sit at nine of 12 in the log, and a performance against the fourth-ranked French side is vital to climb the standings. With three rounds of the summer window now reaching their conclusion, every point earned today remains critical for the tournament's overall trajectory.

sections.heading: Maintaining momentum after Italy
sections.body: Following a dominant 47-17 victory over Italy, the squad enters the current break with the coaching staff focused on refining set-piece efficiency. The team has utilised this period to consolidate their tactical framework, prioritising conditioning to ensure they remain fresh for the remainder of the Nations Championship. With two wins from two, the focus remains on maintaining high standards of execution across the full 80 minutes.

sections.heading: Camp monitoring squad fitness
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the win against Italy. The medical team continues to monitor players through routine load management and rehabilitation protocols as the side prepares for the upcoming block of fixtures. The current squad remains available for selection, with conditioning staff reporting positive trends as the group works through tactical drills during this training phase.

sections.heading: Scotland's physical approach
sections.body: Scotland remains a dangerous side, particularly when relying on their set-piece power and maul threat. Their forwards proved persistent in recent fixtures, consistently challenging opponents through high-impact phases. As we look toward our encounter in November, the focus will be on nullifying their ability to grind out territory and controlling the breakdown to prevent their pack from building the momentum required to execute their driving game.

sections.heading: Building on early success
sections.body: Having secured two victories to date, the team occupies a strong position as the tournament progresses. Every match in the Nations Championship remains critical, and maintaining this winning trajectory is essential to stay at the front of the pack. With the points difference currently at plus 32, the upcoming fixtures will be vital in cementing our standing for the final rounds of competition.

sections.heading: Erasmus rotates squad for Durban encounter
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has rung the changes for today’s fixture, making 10 alterations to the side that overcame Scotland. The selection features 4 Test debutants, with wing Jaco Williams, fly-half Vusi Moyo, lock Ruben van Heerden, and prop Carlu Sadie all included in the starting lineup. Pieter-Steph du Toit will lead the team, which looks to maintain its momentum in the tournament.

sections.heading: Full squad availability reported
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Springbok camp. The coaching staff has focused heavily on integrating the newer members of the squad and managing the tactical transition for this afternoon's contest. The management team appears satisfied with the health and conditioning of the group as they prepare for the physical challenge posed by the Welsh forward pack.

sections.heading: Tandy names new midfield to counter Boks
sections.body: Wales head coach Steve Tandy has made 6 changes to his starting XV, including an entirely new midfield pairing. Captain Dewi Lake has been cleared to lead the side despite earlier concerns, while the visitors have opted for a 6-2 bench split to combat South African physicality. Assistant coach Mzwandile Stick noted that Wales’ current development phase mirrors the Boks' own transition period in 2018.

sections.heading: Unbeaten start on the line
sections.body: South Africa enters today’s match seeking a third consecutive victory to strengthen their position at the top of the standings. Having already secured wins against England and Scotland, the hosts are looking to extend their dominance over Wales, having emerged victorious in each of the last 5 encounters between the two nations across all competitions since late 2024.
